sentence_reviews.text: Curriculum and mixture schedules control which examples, domains, difficulties, or modalities are sampled at each phase of training.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Existing ledger for active_curriculum_learning cites AIMA active learning and learning-from-examples topics; Hoffmann arXiv abstract lines 39-42 discusses model size, number of tokens, training data, and compute-optimal token allocation. No inspected source directly covers multimodal mixture schedules.
sentence_reviews.notes: Example/domain/difficulty scheduling is defensible from active/curriculum and data-token allocation evidence; modalities are broader.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Curricula, data mixtures & sampling schedules is to connect Active learning & curriculum learning, Data collection, curation & filtering to downstream capabilities including Domain randomization & simulation curricula.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Existing ledger lines for active_curriculum_learning support active learning and learning examples, while data_curation ledger entries support dataset/data-management context; domain randomization and simulation curricula are not directly supported by inspected sources.
sentence_reviews.notes: Upstream learning/data connections are supported, but downstream domain-randomization/simulation claim needs direct evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Domain randomization & simulation curricula
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: No inspected packet or ledger source directly connects curricula/data mixtures to domain randomization or simulation curricula.

sentence_reviews.text: combined with Active learning & curriculum learning
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Existing source_sentence_ledger entries for active_curriculum_learning cite AIMA Chapter 19 learning from examples and active learning plus PML data/validation/sampling topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Active learning is directly supported; curriculum learning and mixture schedules are synthesized from data selection/order.

sentence_reviews.text: Results are sensitive to data quality, hyperparameters, compute budget, random variation, and evaluation contamination. Its direct dependencies include Active learning & curriculum learning, Data collection, curation & filtering.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Hoffmann lines 39-42 supports compute/data-token considerations; existing data_curation ledger entries support data quality/management context. Hyperparameters, random variation, and contamination are not directly evidenced here.
sentence_reviews.notes: Data/compute dependencies are supported; generic limitations are overbroad.

sentence_reviews.text: Curricula, data mixtures & sampling schedules uses Active learning & curriculum learning as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Existing active_curriculum_learning reviews cite AIMA active learning under learning from examples and PML data/sampling topics; they note curriculum learning itself is not directly named in AIMA evidence.
sentence_reviews.notes: Data selection/order supports the dependency, but curriculum learning evidence is partly indirect.

sentence_reviews.text: Curricula, data mixtures & sampling schedules uses Data collection, curation & filtering as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Existing data_curation ledger entries cite dataset/data-management and training methodology context; Hoffmann lines 39-42 supports training-token/data allocation under compute budgets.
sentence_reviews.notes: Data curation/filtering is a defensible core ingredient, though filtering specifics are not directly in Hoffmann.

sentence_reviews.text: Curricula, data mixtures & sampling schedules uses Datasets, sampling & splits as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Existing active_curriculum_learning ledger cites PML data/validation/sampling topics; Hoffmann lines 39-42 discusses number of tokens and training data scaling.
sentence_reviews.notes: Sampling/dataset dependence is directly aligned with the node, but splits are broader than the inspected evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: Curricula, data mixtures & sampling schedules uses Compute-optimal scaling & training allocation as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Hoffmann arXiv abstract lines 39-42 investigates optimal model size and tokens under a compute budget and compute-optimal scaling; Kaplan lines 41-43 supports optimal allocation of fixed compute budget.
sentence_reviews.notes: Compute-optimal allocation supports data/token schedule decisions; common strength is defensible.

edge_reviews.notes: Active data selection supports this relationship; core strength is defensible for curricula but evidence for curriculum learning itself is indirect.
Data mixture/schedule design depends on collected and curated data; core strength is appropriate.
Sampling and token/data allocation evidence supports a common datasets/sampling dependency.
Compute-optimal token/data allocation supports a common relationship to training allocation.
